skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Creates detailed implementation plans for completed designs, including tasks, file paths, example code, commands, and verification steps.
skill.summary: Turn completed designs into step-by-step implementation plans for engineers.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives from Markdown fences, inline paths, and example commands in SKILL.md. Weak cryptographic algorithm findings are also false positives triggered by ordinary words and .md paths, with no cryptographic code or malicious intent found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the referenced content is Markdown formatting and example commands. The skill does not include runnable scripts or code that executes commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is contained in Markdown fences, inline file paths, and example pytest or git commands. No script file, command runner, or user-input shell interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines contain natural language and Markdown path text. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not reference MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or crypto APIs. The matches appear to come from substrings such as description text and .md file paths.

content.value_statement: Complex designs often fail during handoff because engineers lack context, file paths, and verification steps. This skill turns a completed design into a clear implementation plan with tasks, examples, and test guidance.
actual_capabilities: Creates plan documents with a goal, architecture summary, and technology stack section.
Breaks implementation work into small tasks that include files, steps, tests, and commits.
Specifies exact file paths and line ranges for planned changes when context is available.
Includes complete example code and expected test outcomes in the plan.
Adds a handoff section with execution options for continuing the work.
limitations: Requires a completed design or clear feature scope before planning.
Does not inspect code unless the assistant using it has repository access.
Generated commands are examples and should be reviewed before execution.
Plan quality depends on the accuracy of the supplied project context.

use_cases.title: Prepare Engineering Handoffs
use_cases.description: Convert an approved design into actionable tasks for an engineer who has limited codebase context.

use_cases.title: Plan Test-Driven Feature Work
use_cases.description: Create a plan that starts with failing tests, adds minimal implementation, and verifies each step.

use_cases.title: Standardize AI Coding Sessions
use_cases.description: Produce structured plans that can be handed to Claude, Codex, or Claude Code for later execution.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-plans skill to create an implementation plan for this completed design: [paste design]. Include tasks, files, tests, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Feature With Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a detailed implementation plan for [feature]. Use test-first steps, include expected failing and passing results, and identify exact files to create or modify.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Handoff Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a plan for an engineer with no project context. Include architecture notes, file paths, code examples, commands, expected output, and commit checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Execution-Ready Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an execution-ready plan for [large change]. Split work into 2-5 minute steps, include dependencies between tasks, verification gates, and a final handoff section.

output_examples.input: Plan a new user settings page after the design is approved.
output_examples.output: A document with the feature goal, architecture approach, files to create or modify, test-first tasks, verification commands, and commit points.

output_examples.input: Turn this API validation design into work for another engineer.
output_examples.output: A task-by-task implementation plan covering tests, validation logic, documentation updates, expected failures, expected passes, and final review steps.

output_examples.input: Prepare a plan that another AI coding session can execute.
output_examples.output: A saved plan with clear scope, small steps, exact file references, execution choices, and a handoff to the next planning or execution skill.

best_practices: Provide the completed design, relevant repository context, and target test command before asking for the plan.
Review generated commands and file paths before using the plan for execution.
Keep each task small enough for one focused implementation and verification cycle.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill before the design or feature scope is stable.
Do not accept vague tasks that omit files, tests, or expected results.
Do not execute generated shell commands without checking project context first.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates detailed implementation plans with goals, architecture notes, task steps, file paths, code examples, tests, and handoff guidance.

faq.question: Does it write production code directly?
faq.answer: No. It writes a plan that another engineer or AI coding session can use to implement the work.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is written for AI-assisted planning and lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It includes example commands in the generated plan, but execution depends on the user or assistant running the plan.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the completed design, project constraints, relevant files, test expectations, and any preferred implementation order.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it when the feature is still being brainstormed or when there is not enough project context to name files and tests.
